jobs.title: MLT: Toxicology & Infectious Disease Lab
jobs.description:
Monarch Diagnostics is seeking a motivated Medical Laboratory Technician (MLT) to join our team in Irvine, CA. This full-time, in-person role supports toxicology and infectious disease testing, ensuring high-quality results to aid patient care.The ideal candidate holds an Associate’s degree in Clinical Laboratory Science or related field, with current MLT certification (ASCP/AMT or equivalent) and strong attention to detail. Prior clinical lab experience is preferred.#J-18808-Ljbffr
Qualifications
The ideal candidate holds an Associate’s degree in Clinical Laboratory Science or related field, with current MLT certification (ASCP/AMT or equivalent) and strong attention to detail
Responsibilities
This full-time, in-person role supports toxicology and infectious disease testing, ensuring high-quality results to aid patient care

jobs.title: Environmental Science Specialist
jobs.description:
Job Title: Environmental Science Specialist

Job Description: We are looking for a dedicated and knowledgeable Environmental Science Specialist to join our team. In this role, you will play a critical part in ensuring compliance with environmental laws and regulations while enhancing the safety of the work environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Assist in the development of training materials and curricula.Provide expert advisory services on a range of environmental issues and projects.Act as a key consultant on environmental compliance in your area of expertise.Ensure adherence to health, safety, and environmental procedures.Contribute to environmental projects by applying your knowledge and skills in biological sciences.If you are passionate about environmental science and making a positive impact, we encourage you to apply!
Responsibilities
In this role, you will play a critical part in ensuring compliance with environmental laws and regulations while enhancing the safety of the work environment
Assist in the development of training materials and curricula
Provide expert advisory services on a range of environmental issues and projects
Act as a key consultant on environmental compliance in your area of expertise
Ensure adherence to health, safety, and environmental procedures
Contribute to environmental projects by applying your knowledge and skills in biological sciences

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
Environmental Scientist Emeryville, CA Start Date 07/20/2026
Overview Salary Range $96,533.00 - $110,336.20 Salary/year Position Type Full Time Education Level Bachelors Degree Travel Percentage Negligible
Description Name of Employer: GSI Environmental Inc.
Position Location: 2000 Powell Street, Suite 820, Emeryville, CA 94608.
Telecommuting is permitted, but applicants must live within a reasonable commuting distance.
Salary: $96,533.00 - $110,336.20 per year
Hours: Monday - Friday, 8:00 am to 5:00 pm
Summary of Duties:
Research, acquire, compile, and manage data for environmental projects.
Evaluate environmental project data and information to assess site condition.
Coordinate and oversee field and laboratory programs.
Perform computer modeling and data analysis to develop solutions to environmental problems.
Prepare documentations and reports.
Assist PTLs and Principals with preparation of proposals (scopes of work and budgets).
Participate in environmental project team meetings.
Develop professional network by participating in professional societies and conference attendance.
Qualifications Education requirements: Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Environmental Engineering, or related field or foreign equivalent.
Experience requirements: 2 years of experience in the job offered or related role.
Special Skills or Other Requirements: 2 years of experience must include:
Landslide and debris flow simulation software, civil engineering, and geological hazard assessment.
Hydrologic and hydraulic (H&H) modeling for flood mapping, groundwater modeling, and sediment transport analysis.
Statistical analysis of large climate datasets, including trend analysis, Bayesian approach, and climate change analysis.
Machine learning and deep learning models to analyze groundwater level and streamflow, spatial data, and satellite images.
Developing and applying numerical models (Finite Difference/Finite Volume) for hydraulic and structural integrity analysis for dam safety and breach modeling.
Unsupervised clustering and classification techniques for geospatial data, including soil types and geological mapping.
Freshwater modeling.
Travel requirements: 5% domestic travel and 5% international travel.
Qualifications
Qualifications Education requirements: Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering, Environmental Engineering, or related field or foreign equivalent
Experience requirements: 2 years of experience in the job offered or related role
Special Skills or Other Requirements: 2 years of experience must include:
Landslide and debris flow simulation software, civil engineering, and geological hazard assessment
Travel requirements: 5% domestic travel and 5% international travel
Benefits
